summary.intent: Fix review comments from PR #506 on the moltis repo, specifically improving a test that was passing both before and after the fix (and thus not actually pinning the new behavior).
summary.outcome: Replaced the ineffective third test with one that verifies the realistic post-fix scenario: explicit models get registered correctly with an empty prefetched map, confirming discovery is not fired. Changes committed and pushed.

repo: register_custom_providers merges prefetched discovery results even when explicit models exist, because fetch_models defaults to true — but in practice the prefetched map is empty because fire_discoveries skips providers with explicit models.
merge_preferred_and_discovered_models puts preferred models first and deduplicates, so merging an empty prefetched map is a no-op.
The real behavioral fix is that fire_discoveries skips providers with explicit models (no HTTP call); the first existing test already covers this path.

code.finding: from_config_with_prefetched accepts a HashMap of DiscoveredModel and is the entry point for testing prefetch/discovery integration without making HTTP calls.

workflow: A test that passes both before and after a fix provides no value — tests should be written to fail on the old behavior and pass only with the fix.
When a guard controls whether an HTTP call is made (fire_discoveries) but not whether cached/passed results are merged (register_custom_providers), tests must target the right layer to be meaningful.

friction: Initial third test was semantically incorrect — it passed pre-fix, making it useless as a regression guard, requiring a redesign of the test approach mid-PR.
Had to carefully reason through two separate code paths (fire_discoveries vs register_custom_providers) to understand where the real fix was applied before writing a valid test.
open_items: register_custom_providers still merges prefetched results even when explicit models are present — this is currently benign but could be an unexpected behavior if a non-empty prefetched map is passed for a provider that has explicit models.
